name.description: The name to give to this interface.

interface-inet-address.description: Selects a specific IP address, which can be public, private, or loopback. This is the default network interface.

interface-any-address.description: Uses the INADDR_ANY wildcard address which will listen for inbound client requests on all interfaces.

interface-global.description: Selects a global (non-loopback, non-site-local, non-link-local) IP address in an IPv4 or IPv6 address block.

interface-link-local.description: Selects a link-local (private) IP address in an IPv4 or IPv6 address block.

interface-loopback.description: Selects an IP address in an IPv4 or IPv6 loopback address block.

interface-non-loopback.description: Selects an IP address in an IPv4 or IPv6 non-loopback address block.

interface-site-local.description: Selects a site-local (private) IP address in an IPv4 or IPv6 address block.

interface-match-host.description: Matches a host name using a regular expression.

value.description: A regular expression used to match a host name. For example 'a-z]+\.mydomain'.

interface-match-interface.description: Matches an interface name using a regular expression.

value.description: A regular expression used to match an interface name. For example 'eth[0-9]+'.

interface-match-address.description: Matches an interface address using a regular expression.

value.description: A regular expression used to match an interface address. For example '10\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+'.

default-interface.description: The network interface used by default.

port-offset.description: An optional offset to add to all the ports associated with this socket binding.

name.description: A unique name for this socket binding.

port.description: The network port to associate with this socket binding.

interface.description: An optional network interface to use for this binding. If not specified, the socket-bindings.default-interface will be used.

name.description: Name for the datasource (used for management).

driver.description: Unique reference to the JDBC driver.

transaction-isolation.description: Set the java.sql.Connection transaction isolation level to use. Defaults to READ_COMMITTED.

new-connection-sql.description: SQL statement to be executed on a connection after creation.

username.description: Username to use for basic authentication with the database.

password.description: Password to use for basic authentication with the database.

max-size.description: Maximum number of connections in the pool.

min-size.description: Minimum number of connections the pool should hold.

initial-size.description: Initial number of connections the pool should hold.

blocking-timeout.description: Maximum time to block while waiting for a connection before throwing an exception. Default is 0 meaning that a call will wait indefinitely. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

background-validation.description: Time between background validation runs. A duration of 0 means this feature is disabled. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

validate-on-acquisition.description: Connections idle for longer than this time are validated before being acquired (foreground validation). A duration of 0 means this feature is disabled. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

leak-detection.description: Time a connection has to be held before a leak warning. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

idle-removal.description: Time a connection has to be idle before it can be removed. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

name.description: Specifies the name of the credential keystore.

path.description: File name of the credential keystore. If the path is relative, the full path will be resolved using the 'relative-to' attribute.

relative-to.description: A property name whose value will be used to resolve relative paths.

type.description: The type of the credential store file. Can be either pkcs12 or jceks. Defaults to pkcs12.

masked-credential.description: Adds a masked password for the credential keystore.

masked.description: Specifies a masked password in the format of 'MASKED_VALUE;SALT;ITERATION'.

command-credential.description: Executes an external command that supplies the password for the credential keystore.

command.description: An external command, including arguments, that returns the credential on the standard output.

credential-reference.description: Credential reference to be used by the configuration.

store.description: Credential store name used to fetch credential with given 'alias' from.

alias.description: Alias of credential in the credential store.

name.description: The name of the security realm.

default-realm.description: Specifies which of the underlying realms will be used by default. It defaults to the first realm.

cache-max-size.description: The maximum size for the identity cache for this realm. If the size is less than 1, the cache will be disabled. Defaults to 256.

cache-lifespan.description: The lifespan of entries in the identity cache after which they expire and are reloaded from the realm provider. Defaults to 60s.

x500-subject-evidence-decoder.description: An EvidenceDecoder that derives the principal associated with the given evidence from the subject from the first certificate in the certificate chain.

x509-subject-alt-name-evidence-decoder.description: An EvidenceDecoder that derives the principal associated with the given evidence from an X.509 subject alternative name from the first certificate in the given evidence.

alt-name-type.description: The subject alternative name type to decode from the given evidence.

segment.description: The 0-based occurrence of the subject alternative name to map. Only used when there is more than one subject alternative name of the given alt-name-type.

path.description: Specifies the location of the keystore on the host file system.

relative-to.description: Specifies a property name that resolves to a directory on the host file system.

password.description: The password required to open the keystore.

alias.description: The alias of the entry in the keystore to use as the server identity.

key-password.description: A password required to access a specific entry within the keystore.

generate-self-signed-certificate-host.description: If set and the keystore file does not exist, a self-signed certificate will be generated on first use. The value is used for the Common Name. Intended for testing only.

provider.description: The name of the provider to use to instantiate the KeyManagerFactory.

type.description: The type of the keystore. Normally auto-detected.

path.description: Specifies the location of the truststore on the host file system.

relative-to.description: Specifies a property name that resolves to a directory on the host file system.

password.description: The password required to open the truststore.

provider.description: The name of the provider to use to instantiate the TrustManagerFactory.

type.description: The type of the truststore. Normally auto-detected.

enabled-protocols.description: A space-separated list of SSL protocol names to enable in order of preference. For example, 'TLSv1.3'.

enabled-ciphersuites.description: The filter to be applied to the cipher suites made available by this SSL engine.

enabled-ciphersuites-tls13.description: The cipher suite names to use for the TLSv1.3 engine.

principal.description: Specifies the principal that the KeyTab represents.

keytab-path.description: Sets the path to the KeyTab for retrieving credentials.

relative-to.description: Specifies the name of a named path or a standard path that the system provides.

minimum-remaining-lifetime.description: Specifies, in seconds, how long a cached credential can remain before it is recreated.

request-lifetime.description: Specifies, in seconds, how much lifetime to request for newly created credentials.

fail-cache.description: Specifies the amount of time, in seconds, to wait before attempting to obtain server credential if the previous attempt failed.

server.description: Specifies if the realm is server-side (default) or client-side.

obtain-kerberos-ticket.description: Controls if a KerberosTicket is also obtained and associated with the credential.

debug.description: Defines if the JAAS step to obtain the credential has debug logging enabled.

wrap-gss-credential.description: Specifies if generated GSS credentials are wrapped to prevent improper disposal.

required.description: Specifies if the keytab file with adequate principal must exist when the service starts.

mechanism-names.description: Defines the mechanism names with which the credential can be used. Space-separated list.

mechanism-oids.description: Defines the mechanism OIDs with which the credential can be used. Space-separated list.

name.description: Names the security realm to logically separate multiple realms of the same type.

url.description: Specifies the URL for LDAP server connections in the format ldap[s]://{hostname}:{port}.

principal.description: Specifies the user principal for LDAP server connections.

credential.description: Specifies the user credential for LDAP server connections.

direct-verification.description: Configures the realm to verify credentials by connecting to LDAP servers with the account.

page-size.description: Sets the page size for realm iteration. The default value is 50.

referral-mode.description: Specifies if LDAP server referrals are followed. Values are 'ignore', 'follow' (default), and 'throw'.

connection-timeout.description: Sets the timeout for LDAP server connections. Default is 5s. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

read-timeout.description: Sets the read timeout for LDAP server operations. Default is 1m. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

client-ssl-context.description: The name of a realm which provides a trust store with which to validate SSL client connections.

name-rewriter.description: Specifies a name rewriter that transforms names returned by a realm.

case-principal-transformer.description: Specifies a PrincipalTransformer definition using case conversion.

name.description: Deprecated. Will be ignored.

uppercase.description: Whether to transform to UPPERCASE or lowercase. The default is true.

common-name-principal-transformer.description: Specifies a PrincipalTransformer definition which extracts the first CommonName (CN) from a DistinguishedName (DN).

regex-principal-transformer.description: Specifies a PrincipalTransformer definition using regular expressions and Matcher based replacement.

name.description: Deprecated. Will be ignored.

pattern.description: Specifies the regular expression for this PrincipalTransformer.

replacement.description: Specifies the replacement string for the PrincipalTransformer.

replace-all.description: Replaces all occurrences instead of the first occurrence.

ldap-identity-mapping.description: Specifies configuration options that define how principals are mapped to corresponding entries in LDAP servers.

search-dn.description: Names the context for query execution.

rdn-identifier.description: Specifies an LDAP attribute that contains the user name and appears in the path of new entries.

search-time-limit.description: The time limit of LDAP search in milliseconds. Defaults to 10000 ms.

filter-name.description: Specifies the LDAP filter that retrieves an identity by name.

ldap-attribute-mapping.description: Specifies the attribute mappings defined for this resource.

filter.description: The filter to use to obtain the values for a specific attribute. String '{0}' will be replaced by username, '{1}' by user identity DN.

filter-dn.description: The name of the context where the filter should be performed.

from.description: The name of the LDAP attribute to map to an identity attribute.

to.description: The name of the identity attribute mapped from a specific LDAP attribute.

search-recursive.description: Indicates if attribute LDAP search queries are recursive.

role-recursion.description: Sets recursive roles assignment - value determines maximum depth of recursion. (0 for no recursion)

role-recursion-name.description: Determines LDAP attribute of role entry which will be substituted for '{0}' in filter-name when searching roles of role.

extract-rdn.description: The RDN key to use as the value for an attribute, in case the value in its raw form is in X.500 format.

reference.description: The name of an LDAP attribute containing DN of entry to obtain value from.

filter-dn.description: The name of the context where the filter should be performed.

from.description: The name of the LDAP attribute to map to an identity attribute.

to.description: The name of the identity attribute mapped from a specific LDAP attribute.

search-recursive.description: Indicates if attribute LDAP search queries are recursive.

role-recursion.description: Sets recursive roles assignment - value determines maximum depth of recursion. (0 for no recursion)

role-recursion-name.description: Determines LDAP attribute of role entry which will be substituted for '{0}' in filter-name when searching roles of role.

extract-rdn.description: The RDN key to use as the value for an attribute, in case the value in its raw form is in X.500 format.

ldap-user-password-mapper.description: Specifies the user password credential mapping defined for this resource.

from.description: The name of the LDAP attribute to map to an identity user password credential.

verifiable.description: If the password credential is verifiable.

name.description: Names the security realm to logically separate multiple realms of the same type.
Names the security realm to logically separate multiple realms of the same type.

path.description: Path to the user properties file.

relative-to.description: Base path for resolving relative paths.

plain-text.description: Whether passwords are stored in plain text.

path.description: The path to the properties file to use for user to group mapping.

relative-to.description: The name of a system property which will be used to resolve the path to the group properties file.

name.description: Names the security realm to logically separate multiple realms of the same type.

auth-server-url.description: The URL of the authentication server.

principal-claim.description: The claim to use as the principal.

issuer.description: Space-separated list of allowed issuers for JWT tokens.

audience.description: Space-separated list of audiences supported by this configuration.

public-key.description: A default public key in PEM format used to validate the signature of tokens without kid header parameter.

jku-timeout.description: A timeout for cached jwks when using jku claim. Default is 2m. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

connection-timeout.description: Sets the timeout for connections to the JKU server. Default is 2s. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

read-timeout.description: Sets the read timeout for the JKU server. Default is 2s. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

client-ssl-context.description: The name of a realm which provides a trust store with which to validate SSL client connections.

host-name-verification-policy.description: A HostnameVerifier that will be used to validate the hostname when using SSL/TLS. Can be ANY or DEFAULT.

client-id.description: The identifier of a client registered within the OAuth2 Authorization Server.

client-secret.description: The secret of the client identified by the given clientId.

introspection-url.description: An URL pointing to a RFC-7662 OAuth2 Token Introspection compatible endpoint.

connection-timeout.description: Sets the timeout for connections to the OAuth2 server. Default is 2s. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

read-timeout.description: Sets the read timeout for the OAuth2 server. Default is 2s. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

client-ssl-context.description: The name of a realm which provides a trust store with which to validate SSL client connections.

host-name-verification-policy.description: A HostnameVerifier that will be used to validate the hostname when using SSL/TLS. Can be ANY or DEFAULT.

name.description: Names the security realm to logically separate multiple realms of the same type.

security-realm-distributed.description: A realm definition for authentication and authorization of identities distributed between multiple realms.

name.description: Names the security realm to logically separate multiple realms of the same type.

realms.description: A space-separated list of security realms that should be used for authentication until one succeeds.

security-realm-aggregate.description: A realm definition for authentication and authorization of identities aggregated between multiple realms.

name.description: Names the security realm to logically separate multiple realms of the same type.

authentication-realm.description: The name of a security realm that should be used for authentication.

authorization-realms.description: A space-separated list of security realm names that should be used for authorization.

socket-binding.description: Specifies the socket the endpoint connector binds to.

security-realm.description: Names the security realm to use for authentication, cache authorization, and encryption.

socket-binding.description: Specifies the socket the endpoint connector binds to.

security-realm.description: Names the security realm to use for authentication, cache authorization, and encryption.

admin.description: Enable administrative features on this endpoint. Defaults to true.

metrics-auth.description: Enable metrics authentication on this endpoint. Defaults to true.

io-threads.description: Sets the number of I/O threads. Defaults to 2 * cpu cores.

idle-timeout.description: Specifies the maximum time that client connections can remain inactive. Defaults to 0 (no timeout). Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

tcp-nodelay.description: Enables TCP NODELAY on the TCP stack.

tcp-keepalive.description: Enables TCP KEEPALIVE on the TCP stack.

send-buffer-size.description: Sets the size of the send buffer.

receive-buffer-size.description: Sets the size of the receive buffer.

require-ssl-client-auth.description: Requires clients to use certificates for authentication.

from.description: A network mask using CIDR notation.

socket-binding.description: Specifies the socket this connector binds to.

io-threads.description: Sets the number of I/O threads.

idle-timeout.description: Specifies the maximum time that client connections can remain inactive. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

tcp-nodelay.description: Enables TCP NODELAY on the TCP stack.

tcp-keepalive.description: Enables TCP KEEPALIVE on the TCP stack.

send-buffer-size.description: Sets the size of the send buffer.

receive-buffer-size.description: Sets the size of the receive buffer.

require-ssl-client-auth.description: Requires clients to use certificates for authentication.

name.description: Logically names this connector.

socket-binding.description: Specifies the socket this connector binds to.

io-threads.description: Sets the number of I/O threads.

idle-timeout.description: Specifies the maximum time that client connections can remain inactive. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

tcp-nodelay.description: Enables TCP NODELAY on the TCP stack.

tcp-keepalive.description: Enables TCP KEEPALIVE on the TCP stack.

send-buffer-size.description: Sets the size of the send buffer.

receive-buffer-size.description: Sets the size of the receive buffer.

require-ssl-client-auth.description: Requires clients to use certificates for authentication.

external-host.description: Sets an external address for this node to accept client connections.

external-port.description: Sets an external port for this node.

max-content-length.description: Sets the maximum size a request from a remote client can be.

security-realm.description: Names the security realm to use for authentication, cache authorization, and encryption.

network-prefix-override.description: Configures whether to use the netmask that the host system provides for interfaces or override with netmasks that follow IANA private address conventions.

name.description: Logically names this connector.

socket-binding.description: Specifies the socket this connector binds to.

io-threads.description: Sets the number of I/O threads.

idle-timeout.description: Specifies the maximum time that client connections can remain inactive. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

tcp-nodelay.description: Enables TCP NODELAY on the TCP stack.

tcp-keepalive.description: Enables TCP KEEPALIVE on the TCP stack.

send-buffer-size.description: Sets the size of the send buffer.

receive-buffer-size.description: Sets the size of the receive buffer.

require-ssl-client-auth.description: Requires clients to use certificates for authentication.

context-path.description: Sets the context path for REST connectors. Defaults to the root context.

extended-headers.description: Enables extended headers. Values are NEVER / ON_DEMAND (default).

max-content-length.description: Sets the maximum allowed content length.

compression-level.description: Sets the level for compressed requests and responses.

compression-threshold.description: The response body is compressed when the size exceeds the threshold. 0 will enable compression for all responses.

security-realm.description: Names the security realm to use for authentication, cache authorization, and encryption.

name.description: Logically names this connector.

socket-binding.description: Specifies the socket this connector binds to.

io-threads.description: Sets the number of I/O threads.

idle-timeout.description: Specifies the maximum time that client connections can remain inactive. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

tcp-nodelay.description: Enables TCP NODELAY on the TCP stack.

tcp-keepalive.description: Enables TCP KEEPALIVE on the TCP stack.

send-buffer-size.description: Sets the size of the send buffer.

receive-buffer-size.description: Sets the size of the receive buffer.

require-ssl-client-auth.description: Requires clients to use certificates for authentication.

cache.description: Names the cache that the Memcached connector exposes. Defaults to memcachedCache.

client-encoding.description: Sets client encoding for values. Applies to memcached text protocol only.

max-content-length.description: Sets the maximum size a request from a remote client can be.

protocol.description: The protocol supported by this connector. One of AUTO (default), TEXT or BINARY.

security-realm.description: Names the security realm to use for authentication, cache authorization, and encryption.

name.description: Logically names this connector.

socket-binding.description: Specifies the socket this connector binds to.

io-threads.description: Sets the number of I/O threads.

idle-timeout.description: Specifies the maximum time that client connections can remain inactive. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

tcp-nodelay.description: Enables TCP NODELAY on the TCP stack.

tcp-keepalive.description: Enables TCP KEEPALIVE on the TCP stack.

send-buffer-size.description: Sets the size of the send buffer.

receive-buffer-size.description: Sets the size of the receive buffer.

require-ssl-client-auth.description: Requires clients to use certificates for authentication.

cache.description: Names the cache that the RESP connector exposes. Defaults to respCache.

max-content-length.description: Sets the maximum size a request from a remote client can be.

security-realm.description: Specifies a security realm for the RESP connector.

topology-state-transfer.description: Topology state transfer configuration for Hot Rod.

lock-timeout.description: Configures lock acquisition timeouts for topology caches. Defaults to 10s. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

replication-timeout.description: Configures replication timeouts for topology caches. Defaults to 10s. Supports time units: ms, s, m, h, d.

await-initial-retrieval.description: Configures whether initial state retrieval should happen immediately at startup.

security-realm.description: Names the security realm to use for authentication and authorization. Deprecated since 12.1.

sasl.description: The configuration of the SASL authentication layer for this server.

server-principal.description: The principal to use as the server identity. Required for Kerberos-based SASL mechs (e.g. GSSAPI, GS2_KRB5).

server-name.description: Names the server that is exposed to clients.

mechanisms.description: A space-separated list of allowed SASL mechanism names.

qop.description: A space-separated list of quality-of-protection values, in decreasing order of preference.

strength.description: A space-separated list of cipher strength values, in decreasing order of preference.

policy.description: A space-separated list of policies to use to narrow down the available set of mechanisms.

property.description: Additional properties required by the specified mechanisms.

security-realm.description: The security realm to use for authentication/authorization. Deprecated since 12.1.
The security realm to use for authentication/authorization. Deprecated since 12.1.

mechanisms.description: The authentication method to require. Can be NONE, BASIC, DIGEST, CLIENT_CERT, SPNEGO. Defaults to NONE.

server-principal.description: The principal to use as the server identity. Required for Kerberos-based mechs (e.g. SPNEGO).

require-ssl-client-auth.description: Requires clients to use certificates for authentication.

security-realm.description: Names the security realm that contains the SSL keystore. Deprecated since 12.1.

sni.description: An element specifying a TLS SNI mapping.

security-realm.description: A corresponding security realm. If none is specified, the default will be used.

cors-rules.description: Configures CORS (Cross Origin Resource Sharing) for the REST Server.

cors-rule.description: CORS rules that specify the permissions for cross-domain requests based on the origin.
Defines a CORS rule for one or more origins.

name.description: Defines a name for a CORS rule.

allow-credentials.description: Configures if CORS requests use credentials and sets the CORS 'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials' response header.

max-age-seconds.description: Configures how long CORS preflight request headers can be cached and sets the CORS 'Access-Control-Max-Age' response header.

allowed-origins.description: Specifies the CORS 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header that controls which origins can access resources.

allowed-methods.description: Space-separated list specifying the CORS 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods' header in the preflight response.

allowed-headers.description: Space-separated list specifying the CORS 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers' header in the preflight response.

expose-headers.description: Space-separated list specifying the CORS 'Access-Control-Expose-Headers' header in the preflight response.

name.description: Defines the name of a property.
